annotations.label: Category (objects and morphisms)

anchor.snippet: A category

annotations.note: `class Category` bundles objects with hom-types (via `CategoryStruct`/`Quiver`) along with id, composition, and the three axioms.

annotations.label: Source and target of a morphism

anchor.snippet: There are two objects that are associated to every morphism

annotations.note: Source and target are encoded as the two indices of `Quiver.Hom : V → V → Type v`, written `X ⟶ Y`.

annotations.label: Composition of morphisms

anchor.snippet: Morphisms are equipped with a partial binary operation

annotations.note: `CategoryStruct.comp` provides the (typed) composition `(X ⟶ Y) → (Y ⟶ Z) → (X ⟶ Z)`, notated `f ≫ g`.

annotations.label: Identity morphism axiom

anchor.snippet: there exists a morphism

annotations.note: `CategoryStruct.id` gives `𝟙 X` and `Category.id_comp`/`comp_id` are the left/right identity axioms.

annotations.label: Associativity axiom

anchor.snippet: whenever all the compositions are defined

annotations.note: `Category.assoc` states `(f ≫ g) ≫ h = f ≫ g ≫ h` as a field of the `Category` class.

annotations.label: Concrete category identity and composition

anchor.snippet: For a concrete category

annotations.note: `ConcreteCategory` carries `id_apply` and `comp_apply` lemmas stating identity acts as `id` on the underlying set and composition acts as function composition.

annotations.label: Hom-set

anchor.snippet: The collection of all morphisms

annotations.note: `Quiver.Hom X Y` (`X ⟶ Y`) is the type of morphisms between two objects, the hom-set.

annotations.label: Locally small category

anchor.snippet: is called locally small

annotations.note: `class LocallySmall` directly captures the property that each hom-type is equivalent to one in the given universe.

annotations.label: Monomorphism

anchor.snippet: is called a monomorphism

annotations.note: `class Mono` requires the right-cancellation property `g ≫ f = h ≫ f → g = h`.

annotations.label: Split monomorphism / retraction

anchor.snippet: has a left inverse or is a split monomorphism

annotations.note: `structure SplitMono` bundles a retraction with `f ≫ retraction f = 𝟙 X`; the propositional version is `IsSplitMono`.

annotations.label: Left inverse implies monomorphism

anchor.snippet: Morphisms with left inverses are always monomorphisms

annotations.note: `SplitMono.mono` (and the instance `IsSplitMono.mono`) derive `Mono f` from a splitting on the left.

annotations.label: Injective vs monomorphism in concrete categories

anchor.snippet: In concrete categories

annotations.note: Mathlib proves `mono_iff_injective` separately in many concrete categories (e.g. `ModuleCat`, `Grp`) but has no single statement covering all concrete categories where mono = injective.

annotations.label: Epimorphism

anchor.snippet: is called an epimorphism

annotations.note: `class Epi` requires the left-cancellation property `f ≫ g = f ≫ h → g = h`.

annotations.label: Split epimorphism / section

anchor.snippet: has a right inverse or is a split epimorphism

annotations.note: `structure SplitEpi` bundles a section with `section_ ≫ f = 𝟙 Y`; the propositional version is `IsSplitEpi`.

annotations.label: Right inverse implies epimorphism

anchor.snippet: Morphisms having a right inverse are always epimorphisms

annotations.note: `SplitEpi.epi` and the instance `IsSplitEpi.epi` produce `Epi f` from any splitting on the right.

annotations.label: Split monomorphism induces split epimorphism

anchor.snippet: If a monomorphism

annotations.note: `SplitMono.splitEpi` / `SplitEpi.splitMono` exhibit each splitting's partner as the dual splitting on the retraction/section.

annotations.label: Surjection has section equivalent to axiom of choice

anchor.snippet: the statement that every surjection has a section is equivalent to the axiom of choice

annotations.note: `Function.surjective_iff_hasRightInverse` gives one direction using `Classical.choice`, but the equivalence with the axiom of choice itself is not formalized as a metatheorem.

annotations.label: Bimorphism

anchor.snippet: is called a bimorphism

annotations.note: No `Bimorphism` definition was found in Mathlib (only `Mono` and `Epi` separately).

annotations.label: Vector spaces: mono/epi coincide with inj/surj

anchor.snippet: in the category of vector spaces over a fixed field

annotations.note: `ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective` and `ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective` cover vector spaces as the special case where the base ring is a field.

annotations.label: Commutative rings: non-surjective epimorphism

anchor.snippet: In the category of commutative rings

annotations.note: `CommRingCat.epi_iff_epi` characterizes epis in `CommRingCat` (not by surjectivity), but the specific example ℤ → ℚ as a non-surjective epi is not stated.

annotations.label: Isomorphism

anchor.snippet: is called an isomorphism

annotations.note: `structure Iso` bundles a two-sided inverse and `class IsIso` is the propositional version.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of inverse

anchor.snippet: Inverse morphisms, if they exist, are unique

annotations.note: `inv_eq_of_hom_inv_id` (and `inv_eq_of_inv_hom_id`) show any morphism splitting `f` on either side equals `inv f`.

annotations.label: Bimorphism need not be isomorphism

anchor.snippet: While every isomorphism is a bimorphism

annotations.note: Without a `Bimorphism` notion in Mathlib, the statement that bimorphisms need not be isomorphisms is not stated.

annotations.label: Epi + split mono is isomorphism

anchor.snippet: any morphism that is both an epimorphism and a split monomorphism

annotations.note: `isIso_of_epi_of_isSplitMono` (dual: `isIso_of_mono_of_isSplitEpi`) gives `IsIso f` from `Epi f` together with `IsSplitMono f`.

annotations.label: Balanced category

anchor.snippet: is known as a balanced category

annotations.note: `class Balanced` asserts that every morphism that is both mono and epi is an isomorphism.

annotations.label: Endomorphism

anchor.snippet: is an endomorphism

annotations.note: `End X := X ⟶ X` defines endomorphisms and is given a monoid structure.

annotations.label: Split endomorphism

anchor.snippet: A split endomorphism is an idempotent endomorphism

annotations.note: Mathlib has the related Karoubi envelope and `Idempotents` infrastructure but no specific `SplitEndomorphism` definition matching the article.

annotations.label: Automorphism

anchor.snippet: An automorphism is a morphism that is both an endomorphism and an isomorphism

annotations.note: `Aut X := X ≅ X` defines automorphisms as self-isomorphisms.

annotations.label: Automorphism group

anchor.snippet: the automorphisms of an object always form a group

annotations.note: The `instance : Group (Aut X)` makes the automorphisms of any object a group.

annotations.label: Morphisms of algebraic structures

anchor.snippet: For algebraic structures commonly considered in algebra

annotations.note: Mathlib supplies bundled categories like `Grp`, `Ring`, `CommRingCat`, `ModuleCat` whose morphisms are the corresponding structure-preserving homomorphisms.

annotations.label: Topological spaces and homeomorphisms

anchor.snippet: In the category of topological spaces

annotations.note: `TopCat` is the category of topological spaces and `isoOfHomeo`/`homeoOfIso` identify its isomorphisms with homeomorphisms.

annotations.label: Smooth manifolds and diffeomorphisms

anchor.snippet: In the category of smooth manifolds

annotations.note: `Diffeomorph` is defined, but Mathlib has no bundled category of smooth manifolds whose morphisms are smooth maps and isomorphisms are diffeomorphisms.

annotations.label: Small categories and functors

anchor.snippet: In the category of small categories

annotations.note: `Cat` is the (large) category of small categories with functors as morphisms via `Cat.category`.

annotations.label: Functor category and natural transformations

anchor.snippet: In a functor category

annotations.note: `Functor.category` instantiates `C ⥤ D` as a category whose morphisms are `NatTrans` (natural transformations).
